insights.headline: BTS EU/UK 2026 is the dominant tour in this sync, but pacing slightly below their 2023 stadium benchmark
insights.detail: BTS's Tottenham Hotspur Stadium dates hit onsale queues of 195,640 (6 Jul) and 177,873 (7 Jul), and EDP views reached 202,249 at +72h on the first date. That's just under the 220,249 onsale queue recorded for BTS's prior Tottenham date in the venue baseline, and well within the venue's historic 220k-533k megastar range but at the low end of it. Munich's Allianz Arena onsale queue (185,701 and 128,067 across two dates) far exceeds the single 2023 K-pop reference point on file for that venue.

insights.headline: Data quality flag: BTS Munich EDP figure looks like a spreadsheet error, not real demand
insights.detail: The BTS Allianz Arena Munich show on 11 Jul jumps from 30,507 EDP views at +24h to 240,493 at +48h - an ~8x jump with no equivalent spike anywhere else in this sync (typical +24h to +48h growth across all other shows is 1.3x-2x). Recommend checking the source cell before using this figure in any downstream reporting.

insights.headline: Copy-paste error: Pussycat Dolls' Oslo date is labelled with a Barcelona venue
insights.detail: The Pussycat Dolls UK/EU 2026 tracker lists the 10 Sep Oslo show's venue as 'Palau Sant Jordi', which is actually a Barcelona arena. City/country (Oslo, NO) are correct, so the venue field alone needs manual correction in the source sheet.

insights.headline: JAY-Z's single Stade de France date has by far the highest presale figure captured this sync
insights.detail: JAY-Z's 10 Sep 2026 Stade de France show shows a presale queue of 200,100 - more than any other presale figure extracted in this run, including BTS and Bad Bunny. Caveat: Stade de France runs France's non-comparable 'active users' sign-up system rather than UK/NL SmartQueue, so this isn't directly comparable to queue figures elsewhere, but it's still a striking standalone number for a single-date show.

insights.headline: JOJI's EU/UK 2026 tour is pacing very low across all 12 dates
insights.detail: Peak onsale queues across JOJI's full run stay under 300 at every stop (Amsterdam highest at 297; Zurich lowest at 13), and most presale queues are in the low hundreds to low thousands. This matches the 'low demand'/'low but quick clear' pattern already on file for JOJI's Co-op Live and O2 shows in the venue baseline - Manchester's figures (612 presale / 156 onsale) match the historic baseline for that show exactly, which is a good cross-validation signal.

insights.headline: Harry Styles' Wembley opener matches his prior benchmark almost exactly
insights.detail: The 12 Jun 2026 Wembley Stadium date posted 336,232 EDP views at +72h - identical to the 'date 1' Wembley figure already recorded in the venue baseline for Harry Styles, a strong consistency check between the two data sources. Demand then fell off steeply on subsequent London dates (173,906 on 13 Jun, down to 60,364-94,975 on the later June dates).

insights.headline: Data quality flag: Xdinary Heroes figures look like a copied KATSEYE tracker template
insights.detail: Three Xdinary Heroes shows (O2 Ritz Manchester, O2 Forum Kentish Town, and Batschkapp Frankfurt) have EDP/ADP figures that are numerically identical, row for row, to KATSEYE's figures at the exact same venues and dates in a separate sheet. This looks like a shared/duplicated tracker template rather than three independently observed shows - worth flagging to whoever owns the Xdinary Heroes sheet.

insights.headline: Bad Bunny's Riyadh Air Metropolitano residency is tracked across two separate reports with different metrics
insights.detail: The same Madrid residency dates (e.g. 30 May, 31 May, 2-15 Jun) appear in both the original 'Los Vecinos/Prod Releases' tracker (hourly EDP/ADP buckets) and a separate 'Additional Seats' tracker (date-range windows around the Grammys and the additional-seat release). Both were kept as distinct records since they capture genuinely different onsale events for the same show dates, but anyone building a single per-show total should be aware there are two entries per date.

insights.headline: Don Toliver: two shows have ADP figures implausibly higher than EDP
insights.detail: Olympiahalle Munich (29 Oct) and Uber Arena Berlin (2 Nov) both show artist-page (ADP) views well above ticket-page (EDP) views for the same show - the reverse of every other row in the sheet. This lines up with a likely column-alignment issue in the per-market ADP block (country-code columns appear offset from their venue rows) rather than genuine demand.

insights.headline: Bruno Mars' two biggest EMEA stadium dates (Paris, Milan) have no view or queue data at all
insights.detail: Stade de France (20-21 Jun) and Stadio San Siro (14 Jul) are both blank in the source tracker - no EDP, ADP, presale, or onsale figures were captured for either date, despite these being two of the largest-capacity stops on the EMEA 2026 tour. Worth a follow-up with whoever owns that tracker, since every other Bruno Mars date has at least partial figures.

insights.headline: The long-standing 'mystery Hilary Duff' anomaly in the baseline data is now resolved
insights.detail: Earlier baseline data flagged several UK/IE shows as an 'unknown artist mislabeled Hilary Duff' anomaly. This sync's read of the actual 'Hilary Duff - UK Arena Tour 2026' source file confirms these are genuine Hilary Duff shows - a coherent 5-city run (Dublin, Cardiff, London, Manchester, Glasgow). A comparable-reference copy of the Dublin date pasted into The Strokes' tracker shows matching EDP figures (986/2,818/4,033/5,796/8,865 across the +3h to +96h buckets), giving good cross-source validation.

insights.headline: NE-YO & AKON is the most thoroughly-tracked headliner tour in this sync
insights.detail: 27 individual show records were extracted from the NE-YO & AKON UK/EU 2026 tracker, the largest per-tour dataset captured this run. That breadth makes it a good candidate as a reference tour for future headliner-tier venue benchmarking, once figures are reviewed for consistency.
